prompt: Trace the 'order-cancellation' feature. The handler OrderController.cancelOrder calls OrderCancellationService.cancel, which calls PaymentService.initiateRefund (uses RestTemplate to POST to payment-service /api/refunds) and EventPublisher.publish(OrderCancelledEvent).

assertions.text: Outbound Calls table includes the POST /api/refunds call to payment-service
The outbound call status is set to 'unresolved' (not resolved -- that happens in the resolve phase)
Background Jobs and Events section includes OrderCancelledEvent as an event-published entry
The target service name is inferred from the outbound call (payment-service)

prompt: Trace the 'user-registration' feature. Entry point: POST /api/users/register -> UserController.register -> UserRegistrationService.register -> EmailValidator.validate -> PasswordEncoder.hash -> UserRepository.save -> EventPublisher.publish(UserRegisteredEvent).

assertions.text: Service Layer Trace lists all steps from UserController through EventPublisher in order
Each step includes a plain-language description of what it does, not just the method name
The trace contains no actual code -- only class.method names and descriptions
Repository/Data Layer section includes UserRepository with its operations and the User entity

prompt: Trace a feature that has 3 entry points and 2 outbound calls discovered.

assertions.text: Frontmatter phase is updated to 'traced'
Frontmatter status is 'complete'
entry_points_count in frontmatter matches the actual number of entry points traced
Trace Log has one entry per traced entry point with timestamps

prompt: Trace the 'report-generation' feature in a Spring Boot repo. Entry point: POST /api/reports/generate -> ReportController.generate -> ReportService.queueGeneration -> publishes ReportRequestedEvent. Also has @Scheduled method ReportCleanupJob.cleanExpiredReports (runs daily) and @KafkaListener ReportCompletedHandler.handle consuming ReportCompletedEvent.

assertions.text: Background Jobs section includes the scheduled ReportCleanupJob
Background Jobs section includes ReportCompletedHandler as an event consumer
Background Jobs section includes ReportRequestedEvent as a published event
Each background job/event has the correct type (scheduled-job, event-consumed, event-published)

prompt: Trace the 'order-management' feature with 4 entry points: GET /api/orders (list), POST /api/orders (create), GET /api/orders/{id} (detail), POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel (cancel). Each has different service layer depth and different outbound calls.

assertions.text: Service Layer Trace has an H3 subsection for each of the 4 entry points
Each H3 subsection is labeled with the HTTP method and path
Different entry points have different trace depths reflecting their actual complexity
Outbound Calls table aggregates all outbound calls from all entry points without duplicates
